name: einstein-his-life-and-universe
description: Walter Isaacson's Einstein: His Life and Universe — a creativity and genius toolkit that explores how Albert Einstein's imagination, nonconformity, and intellectual persistence led to the theory of relativity and transformed modern physics — and what his life teaches us about original thinking, handling fame, and the moral responsibilities of science.
useCase: Learn how one of history's greatest minds worked — from his creative methods and thought experiments to his handling of fame, his moral choices, and the lessons for anyone trying to think differently.
knownRisks: The biography covers complex physics concepts in accessible terms — experts may find some explanations simplified
Einstein's personal relationships, particularly with Mileva Marić, are portrayed honestly and may be uncomfortable
The book was published in 2007 — some details about unpublished letters have since emerged
